recipes.title: Apple Core
recipes.how: Take an apple from an apple tree. You get one after eating the apple. Ordinary wild apple trees are q10. If you find a tree with apples that are below q10 or above q10 it is not wild. Someone planted it. It is not considered polite to chop down apple trees becuase they keep new players from starving. Also,it can be used to feed domesticated animals.
recipes.description: Apple cores are used to plant more seed of::apple trees by placing them in a filled Treeplanter's Pot.

recipes.title: Ash
recipes.how: Baked goods or Items left in a lit oven or Kiln for too long will become ash. Generally, it is the result of user error. However, should you wish to purposefully create ash, bread is the simplest baked good to produce, while honey buns turn into ash quicker and require only one more ingredient than bread (that being honey).
recipes.description: The remains of overcooked food, used to produce a couple of clothing objects.

recipes.title: Backpack
recipes.how: Craft [C] > Leather Working [L] > Backpack [p] It takes 4 pieces of leather and the leather working skill Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: Backpacks use the "back" equipment slot, which is the sixth equipment slot on the right. While equipped, it extends inventory space by 2 vertical bars. As with all items that provide extra storage, these bonuses are cumulative. Because it uses the back slot, it cannot be used simultaneously with a quiver.

recipes.title: Bar of Tin
recipes.description: Tin is a soft metal that is unsuitable for making durable tools or equipment, but it is the only metal that can be used to produce Tankards, which are currently the only drinking vessel that can be used to hold and drink Beer. Aside from this unique use, the main reason to produce tin is as an ingredient in the making of Bronze, which is a much stronger metal that can be used to produce a much greater variety of tools. Tin can also be used in the construction of any tool or building that requires a Bar of Common Metal, such as a Cauldron. In order to make Tin Nuggets you need an Alloying Crucible.

recipes.title: Bar of Wrought Iron
recipes.how: Craft [C] > Metal Working [e] > Wrought Iron [W] Place any number of bars of Cast Iron from 1-9 into the Finery Forge and fuel it with 2 charcoals after a few minutes the cast iron left inside has a 50% chance of turning into either a bloom or slag. At an anvil with a smithy's hammer equipped, one unit of bloom can be converted into a bar of wrought iron with a one-in-three chance. If the bloom does not become a bar of wrought iron, it instead becomes a bar of cast iron. For a character who is neutral on the nature/industry slider, this means that the inputs to a finery forge can become the following with the stated chances: # 1-in-2 chance of becoming slag (50%). # 1-in-3 chance of becoming cast iron (33.3%). # 1-in-6 chance of becoming wrought iron (16.7%). This is after one iteration. Cast iron created this way can be used in the finery forge again with identical chances as in the first iteration. This means that after an infinite number of iterations, the chances of becoming wrought iron are: 1/6 + 1/3 * (1/6 + 1/3 * (1/6 + 1/3 * (...))) = 1/6 * (1/3^0 + 1/3^1 + 1/3^2 + ...) = .25.
recipes.description: Wrought iron, in bar form, can be used to produce a variety of medium-grade metal goods.

recipes.title: Battleaxe of the Twelfth Bay
recipes.how: Craft > Metal Working > Battleaxe of the Twelfth Bay It requires a massive Twelve steel, and two leather. This is considered the most expensive item in the game.
recipes.description: {{infobox object | name = | image = Image:.png | caption = | type = Equipment, Inventory | skillreq = Steelmaking | objectsreq = Bar of Steel x12, Leather x2 | reqby = Nothing |xsize = 2|ysize = 3}} The Battleaxe of the Twelfth Bay (B12, for short) deals 500 base damage. [http://pastebin.com/eyj6qqC8]

recipes.title: Bear Tooth Talisman
recipes.how: Craft [C] > Jewelry [J] > Bear Tooth Talisman [B] +3 Strength +5 Charisma, , '''Q42 Bear Tooth + Q14 Taproot''', '''Q40''': +7 Strength +10 Charisma (Neutral Slider), '''Q42: '''+6 STR and +10 Charisma, '''Q60''': +7 STR and +12 Charisma (Neutral Slider) Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: A piece of equipment that grants a base of 3 Strength and 5 Charisma. The boost you get is also dependant on your civilization/barbarism slider and changes as that slider does. The closer your Personal Beliefs are to civ, the higher the CHA boost will be and vise versa. The quality of the Bear Tooth Talisman is affected by Psyche and Bear Tooth quality. The Quality of the String has no effect on the final quality of the talisman, only the Quality of the tooth matters.

recipes.title: Birchbark
recipes.description: Find a birch tree. They have white tree trunks and yellow leaves. Right click on the tree to bring up the option "Take bark".

recipes.title: Black Tea Leaves
recipes.how: Gained from leaving Fresh Tea Leaves onto a Herbalist Table for approx 8-9 RL hours.
recipes.description: Together with Green Tea Leaves they currently serve the same purpose in making Tea. Because the quality of Black Tea Leaves are influenced by the Herbalist Table twice, the quality of tea is closer to the table than tea from Green Tea Leaves.

recipes.title: Bloody Rag
recipes.description: When you remove gauze from your head, it turns into a Bloody Rag. At the moment the only use for it is to sacrifice rags to ancestors. If you don't plan on aquiring Numen, you might just discard Bloody Rags.

recipes.title: Bone Ash
recipes.how: Burn Bone in a Kiln, it needs 2 branches.
recipes.description: Bone Ash is an ingredient of Bone Clay, which is used to make Porcelain plates.

recipes.title: Bone Clay
recipes.materials: Bone Ash x5, Cave Clay, Feldspar
recipes.description: Bone Clay is currently only used to make Porcelain Plate and Ceramic Meat Grinder.

recipes.title: Bread Dough
recipes.how: Have wheat flour and a container filled with at least 1.0 liter of water in your inventory, then craft bread dough. Craft > Cooking > Baking > Bread
recipes.description: Wheat Flour and Water combined into a dough, ready for baking.

recipes.title: Bronze Nugget
recipes.description: Bronze nuggets are made by using the craft with an Alloying Crucible while holding a Bar of Bronze. This gives 10 Bronze Nuggets, usable in crafting or smeltable back to a bar. The quality of a nugget depends only on the quality of the bar used Qnugget = Qbar.

recipes.title: Bunny Slippers
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Bunny Slippers
recipes.description: are cosy footwear. They provide no armor bonuses, but are the only shoes that are indestructable. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Agility |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +2 |- | style="text-align: center;"|<10 | style="text-align: center;"| +1 |}

recipes.title: Cast Iron Nugget
recipes.description: Cast Iron nuggets are made by using the craft with an Alloying Crucible while holding a Bar of Cast Iron. This gives 10 Cast Iron Nuggets, usable in crafting or smeltable back to a bar. The quality of a nugget depends only on the quality of the bar used Qnugget = Qbar.

recipes.title: Chef's Hat
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Hats > Chef's Hat Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: Wearing a Chef's Hat will boost your cooking skill by 5 plus 1 per 10 quality of the hat (5+x/10 rounded up).

recipes.title: Chicken Egg
recipes.how: Chicken eggs are laid by hens in a chicken coop. It takes roughly 24 hours for a hen to lay an egg. There needs to be at least one empty square directly below the hen, otherwise the egg won't appear. If there is a cock in the coop with the hen, the produced egg will be fertile and will hatch into a chick. Fertile eggs need to be brooded by a hen in order to hatch. To brood, the egg needs to be in a square space directly below the hen.
recipes.description: An egg laid by chicken. Required by several cooking recipes, can be eaten fried or boiled.

recipes.title: Chicken Feather
recipes.how: Butchering a Dead Chicken or a Dead Cock provides you with several materials, including three chicken feathers.
recipes.description: s can be used as quills, which, along with ink and parchment, are used to draw maps. The has a chance of breaking when used in this fashion. They are also required for Feather Fly, Tin Fly, and Sturdy Bed.

recipes.title: Chieftain's Hat
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Hats > Chieftain's Hat ru:Chieftain's Hat Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor
recipes.description: You can only equip this if you are a Chieftain. this gives +3 CHA(1L). A quality 31 hat gives +33 CHA Q10 = 19 CHA

recipes.title: Copper Nugget
recipes.description: Copper nuggets are made by using the craft with an Alloying Crucible while holding a Bar of Copper. This gives 10 Copper Nuggets, usable in crafting or smeltable back to a bar. The quality of a nugget depends only on the quality of the bar used Qnugget = Qbar.

recipes.title: Cured Tobacco Leaf
recipes.how: Gained from a Fresh Tobacco Leaf after drying it on a Herbalist Table for 24 hours real life time.
recipes.description: You need a cured tobacco leaf in order to make Tobacco.

recipes.title: Dawn Chain
recipes.description: +1 Day Personal Belief, Boost to Exploration.

recipes.title: Deer Antlers
recipes.how: Kill a deer, then right click to collect bones on a deer skeleton after skinning and/or butchering the remains. You will get one set of antlers with other bone materials of the same quality. Quality is determined by the formula DeerLevel*5+Survival/2.
recipes.description: The antlers of a deer. Deer Antlers also count as bone material for recipes. {{infobox object | name = | image = Image:.png | xsize = 1 | ysize = 1 | caption = | type = Inventory | skillreq = Unknown | objectsreq = Deer Skeleton }} {{Infobox curiositie | name = | image = Image:.png |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |lpgain = 0 |att = 20 |time = 38hours |producedby = Hand|skillreq = none}}

recipes.title: Dried Hide
recipes.how: Acquired by skinning a cow, deer, aurochs, or boar. Then hanging that skin on a drying frame. Category:Hides
recipes.description: Dried Hide is a generic hide taken from larger animals other than bears. If tanned, you will receive four pieces of leather. There are no items that specifically require a Dried Hide over any other animal hide, but deer hides provide pretty decent quality, which is good for newer players that are in need of decent quality slings and leather armor.

recipes.title: Druid's Helm
recipes.how: Simply, Craft, Metal Working, Armor, Druid's Helm Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor
recipes.description: Druid's Helm is part of the wrought iron armor set. {|width="300" cellspacing="1" cellpadding="1" border="1" !nowrap="nowrap" width="30%" scope="col"|hit points !width="30%" scope="col"|deflect !width="30%" scope="col"|absorb |- |align="center"|225 |align="center"|30 |align="center"|10 |}

recipes.title: Dusk Chain
recipes.description: Boosts Night Personal Belief and Stealth. Night belief boost doesn't depend on quality of Dusk Chain, it will give +1 at any quality. Base Stealth boost is +10.

recipes.title: Family Heirloom
recipes.description: The Family Heirloom is a piece of jewelry, which gives +2 Tradition Personal Belief while worn.

recipes.title: Feather Duster
recipes.how: To acquire a Feather Duster, you must Butcher at least two Chicken Corpses for Feathers, obtain String through Farming or Foraging, and Chop a Log into Blocks, then under Crafting>Curiosities>Feather Duster, Click 'Craft'.
recipes.description: The Feather Duster is a Curiosity that takes 4 attention points and 10 hours to study. Softcapped by Carpentry. After the Feather Duster is studied the player will gain 960 learning points.

recipes.title: Gold Nugget
recipes.description: Gold Nuggets are made from smelting Gold Ore in an Ore Smelter. Gold Nuggets can also be crafted into Bar of Gold and melted back into gold nuggets.

recipes.title: Green Tea Leaves
recipes.how: Gained from Fresh Tea Leaves after having them on a Herbalist Table for half an hour.
recipes.description: Together with Black Tea Leaves they currently serve the same purpose in making Tea.

recipes.title: Hide Cloak
recipes.materials: Dried Bear Hide or Dried Fox Hide or Dried Hide or Dried Sheep Skin or Rabbit Fur
recipes.how: A hide cloak is crafted with a dried hide and has no use other than character customization.Its an item some newbies wear. Despite the game saying it requires a "Dried Cow Hide", a dried hide from any animal will work. Note: It looks like a toga. Category:Equipment Category:Clothing

recipes.title: Kuksa
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Kuksa
recipes.description: This is a type of cup used to gather river or well water, which can be consumed to recover stamina. However, water is less effective at recovering stamina than tea. The Kuksa holds 0.3 L of water and drains hunger while recovering stamina.

recipes.title: Leech
recipes.how: Walk around or stand in a swamp with empty equipment slots, and leeches will soon attach themselves or put them in a basket (any storage will work) and get them there.
recipes.description: Leeches decrease your SHP slowly, but for every point of SHP damage there's a chance of them healing a point of HHP. formula. Each time a leech drains your SHP, its quality will increase or decrease towards your constitution. Once a leech becomes bloated it will no longer have any effect on you, but bloated leeches will become skinny again after sitting in your inventory or a container. It is a good idea to collect leeches and store them for future use.

recipes.title: Malted Wheat
recipes.how: Place germinated wheat seeds in a kiln. Fire for ~5-6 minutes (one branch worth of fuel).
recipes.description: Malted Wheat is Germinated Wheat Seeds heated in a Kiln. It serves no other purpose other than one of the process in making Wort, one of the ingredients for Weißbier.

recipes.title: Militia Sword
recipes.how: Craft > Metal Working > Weapons > Militia Sword Category:Equipment Category:Sharp Tools Category:Weapons
recipes.description: The Militia Sword deals 250 base damage. It is the weakest of the swords.

recipes.title: Miner's Helm
recipes.how: You need to equip a smithy's hammer and you need an anvil to make a Miner's Helm. Craft > Metal Working > Miner's Helm
recipes.description: When a Candle is added to a miner's helm, it becomes a hands-free source of light, perfect for use with a Pickaxe.

recipes.title: Mug
recipes.how: To make a mug, you need the pottery skill and 2 blocks of clay. Craft > Ceramics > ug *Use the craft menu to mold the clay, and then fire the mug in a kiln. *You will get an empty mug. *Requires 3 sticks to fire
recipes.description: A mug is required to drink tea from a tea pot. Mugs have a capacity of 0.3 L. Every 0.1 L of tea restores 15% stamina, hence a mug full of tea will restore a total of 45% stamina.

recipes.title: Pearl Necklace
recipes.description: Increases Learning Ability by 40% at Quality 10. Quality does improve Learning Rate received from the Pearl Necklace. The formula for effect is: sqrt(Necklace Quality/10) * 40% Learning Rate When crafting, string quality is ignored. Instead the average quality of the 20 pearls is used to determine the necklace's quality. *The Learning Ability granted by the necklace is added to your total, rather than multiplied.

recipes.title: Pickaxe
recipes.how: You need to equip a Smithy's Hammer and you need an Anvil to make a pickaxe. q pickaxe = (av. q metal + q branch + q Anvil + q Smithy's Hammer)/4 soft capped by sqrt(strength*smithing) Craft > Metal Working > Pickaxe Category:Equipment Category:Mining Tools
recipes.description: *Increases Mining speed - Reduces stamina drain *Increases Chip Stone Speed - Reduces stamina drain *Allows boulders to be removed from a Cellar so it can be used *Increases the speed things are destroyed - Deals 2 additional damage to buildings.

recipes.title: Poor Man's Belt
recipes.how: Craft [C] > Leather Working [L] > Poor Man's Belt [l] Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: It's key to complete the hobo look, why use anything else when a single strip of leather can make ones junk not flail for the world to see. It has been said that wielding this can increase the clients' speed as the amount of textures needed to draw the graphics are halved via the medium of hefty graphical oddness. However, this has been found to be extremely untrue. Cosmetic. Equips to (3R).

recipes.title: Raw Fox Hide
recipes.how: Equip any sharp tool, then right click on the corpse and select "Skin" from the action list. Category:Hides
recipes.description: The quality of a is affected by survival and the sharp tool quality (ie.: stone axe).

recipes.title: Ring of Brodgar Dough
recipes.materials: Water (0.5 liters), Wheat Flour, Honey (0.1 liter), Poppy Seeds x2
recipes.how: Craft > Cooking > Baking > reads > Ring of Brodgar *1. Light a Cauldron. Wait for it to boil. *2. Have Honey and Water in your inventory (in buckets or other containers), along with Wheat Flour, and 2 handfuls of Poppy Seeds *3. Right-click the cauldron and initiate the recipe. To bake, place the dough in an oven with 3 branches and light it.
recipes.description: You need a cauldron to make a Ring of Brodgar dough.

recipes.title: Roasted Meat
recipes.how: Craft [C] > Cooking [k] > Roasted Meat [R]. Meat can be roasted on a Fire to provide a small food boost, and to provide FEP. FEP provided is QL / 10 * Chicken meat: Dexterity * Boar meat: Strength * Bear meat: Strength * Rabbit meat: Agility * Mutton meat: Charisma * Beef: Constitution * Deer meat: Perception * Fox meat: Intelligence
recipes.description: | image = Image:.png | caption = | type = Inventory |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |lpgain = 5 | skillreq = Wilderness Survival (?) | objectsreq = Raw Meat | producedby = Fire (lit) | reqby = Nothing }}

recipes.title: Saw
recipes.how: Requires the Metal Working skill, one branch, and one Bar of Wrought Iron. Built in the Craft Menu (C) under Tools (T)

recipes.title: Silver Nugget
recipes.description: Silver nuggets are made from smelting Silver Ore in an Ore Smelter. Silver Nuggets can also be crafted into Bar of Silver and melted back into silver nuggets. Silver Nuggets are also used to make Silver for the Ferryman.

recipes.title: Smithy's Hammer
recipes.how: Craft if while using an Anvil. Craft > Metal Working > Smithy's Hammer Category:Equipment
recipes.description: ==How to Acquire== Craft if while using an Anvil. Craft > Metal Working > Smithy's Hammer Category:Equipment

recipes.title: Soldier's Helmet
recipes.how: Craft>Metal Working>Armor>Soldier helmet Q45 Soldier Helmet = Hitpoints 900, Armor class 106/53 Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor
recipes.description: Base (10ql) stats: ~425 Hitpoints, AC 50/25

recipes.title: Sprucecap
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Hats > Sprucecap
recipes.description: Headgear that is that is made from intertwined Fir Boughs. Increases Stealth. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|'''Quality''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Stealth''' |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +2 |- | style="text-align: center;"|40 | style="text-align: center;"| +4 |- | style="text-align: center;"|90 | style="text-align: center;"| +6 |}

recipes.title: Steel Nugget
recipes.description: Steel nuggets are made by using the craft with an Alloying Crucible while holding a Bar of Steel. This gives 10 Steel Nuggets, usable in crafting or smeltable back to a bar. The quality of a nugget depends only on the quality of the bar used Qnugget = Qbar.

recipes.title: Stone
recipes.how: Right-click a Large Stone or Stone Boulder and hit "Chip Stone". Furthermore, you can dig in mountain terrain to get stones. Most stones have a quality of 10, but higher quality stones can be found on mountains and acquired by smelting by-products.
recipes.description: s are used in the making of various items. Also, chipped stones are used as ammunition for Slings.

recipes.title: Tea Pot
recipes.how: To make a tea pot, you need the pottery skill and 4 blocks of clay. Craft > Ceramics > Tea ot *Use the craft menu to mold the clay, and then fire the tea pot in a kiln. *You will get an empty tea pot. * It requires 4 branches to fire it, and can be fired alongside a mug.
recipes.description: Tea pots can hold 1.0 L of tea, but the tea must be poured into a mug in order to drink it.

recipes.title: Tin Nugget
recipes.description: A nugget of tin.Category:ContentCategory:Objects

recipes.title: Treeplanter's Pot
recipes.how: #Gather 10 Clay of any type. #Choose Craft > Ceramics > Treeplanter's Pt, and then click the "Craft" button to get an Unburnt Treeplanter's Pot. #Place the unfinished pot into a kiln with 3+ Branches in it (see Kiln for details on baking pottery). #Right-click the kiln, click "Fire", and wait. #Collect your finished treeplanter's pot after 20 minutes. Tip: Keep in mind that if you don't have room in your inventory for a crafted item or in your cursor it drops to the ground, and if an item drops in the water (where you were gathering the clay for example) you'll lose the item forever. Quality for planted trees depends on the Pot Q, Soil Q, Water Q, and your Farming skill ((Soil+Soil+Soil+Soil/4)+Pot+Water+Seed+table)/5''=Tree Q''
recipes.description: A treeplanter's pot is an important tool used with Soil, Water, a Herbalist Table and any one of the tree "seeds" such as an Apple Core, Fir Cone, or Mulberry to grow a new Tree (full list). The quality is important for treeplanter's pots as it is one of the factors in determining the tree's quality. The quality of an unburnt treeplanter's pot is softcapped by the DEX (dexterity) of the crafter. The quality of the final pot is the average of the quality of the kiln and fuel used with the quality of the unburnt pot doubly weighted.

recipes.title: Waterflask
recipes.how: You will need Leather Working and one piece of Leather. Simply craft it from: Craft -> Tools -> Waterflask
recipes.description: A waterflask made of leather, it is a water container that can hold up to 2 litres of water that could be used for drinking to alleviate some stamina at the cost of some hunger or to be used in a crafting recipe that requires water. The quality of the waterflask does not affect the quality of the water drawn. If there is already water in the waterflask, the quality of the water will be the total average of quality of the water in the waterflask and the water drawn. Waterflasks are the preferred water containers for hearthlings because they store the most water per inventory slot (2.0L / 1 slot) as opposed to other containers such as Kuksas (0.3L / 1 slot) and Waterskins (3.0L / 2 slots).

recipes.title: Wrought Iron Nugget
recipes.description: Wrought Iron nuggets are made by using the craft with an Alloying Crucible while holding a Bar of Wrought Iron. This gives 10 Wrought Iron Nuggets, usable in crafting or smeltable back to a bar. The quality of a nugget depends only on the quality of the bar used Qnugget = Qbar.

recipes.title: Rope
recipes.how: The skill Animal Husbandry is required to craft a rope from ten pieces of string. Craft > Processing & Materials > Sewing & Fabrics > Rope
recipes.description: A rope is a tool used to domesticate animals. It is also required to build a well and other structures.

recipes.title: Wool
recipes.materials: Sheep, or Mouflon and Four-Leaf Clover
recipes.how: You need to equip a Sharp Tool, such as a Stone Axe, in order to shear a Sheep. Mouflons may also be sheared if fed one Four-Leaf Clover for each shearing. The q of the sheared wool is influenced by the q of the cutting tool, the survival skill and either the sewing or sqrt(sewing*dex) skill (tested with thimbles).
recipes.description: is used for several things such as Gauze, Vapntreyiu or Yarn.

recipes.title: Bone Arrow
recipes.how: raft > ools > anged Weapons > Boe Arrow
recipes.description: A bone arrow is ammunition for the Wooden bow and Ranger's Bow. It adds 20 damage and has an IEMM of 2.0 (see Archery). You can keep up to 40 arrows in a quiver by picking up the arrows and right clicking them onto the quiver.

recipes.title: Quiver
recipes.how: A quiver is made from four pieces of leather. Craft > Leather Working > Quiver
recipes.description: A quiver can hold up to 40 arrows (Bone Arrows or Stone Arrows). When equipped, a quiver serves as a medium to draw arrows from when fighting with a bow. '''Note:''' A backpack cannot be equipped at the same time.

recipes.title: Stone Arrow
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Ranged Weapons > Stone Arrow
recipes.description: A stone arrow is ammunition for the Wooden bow and Ranger's Bow. It adds 10 damage and has an IEMM of 2.0 (see Archery). You can keep up to 40 arrows in a quiver by picking up the arrows and right clicking them onto the quiver

recipes.title: Batter
recipes.materials: Jar (Empty), Butter, Wheat Flour, Milk (1.0 L), Chicken Egg
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to make batter. Craft > Cooking > Ingredients > Batter
recipes.description: Batter is a food ingredient, required for Fishsticks and Onion Rings.

recipes.title: River Pearl
recipes.how: Cook a River Pearl Mussel into Boiled River Pearl Mussel, and, very rarely, you will get a pearl in addition to a boiled mussel.
recipes.description: River pearls are extremely rare and valuable items used mainly for crafting jewelry that boosts learning ability.

recipes.title: Beeswax
recipes.how: Beeswax is acquired by right clicking on a Bee Hive and selecting 'harvest wax'. While beehives can contain multiple units of beeswax, if a bee hive has no beeswax, the option will not appear.
recipes.description: Beeswax is required to craft Candles, Coiled Rope Hotpad and Hardened Leather.

recipes.title: Germinated Wheat Seed
recipes.how: Place Wheat Seeds on an Herbalist Table. Wait for them to become Germinated Wheat Seeds.
recipes.description: ==How to Acquire== Place Wheat Seeds on an Herbalist Table. Wait for them to become Germinated Wheat Seeds.

recipes.title: Wheat Grist
recipes.how: raft -> Cooing -> Ingredients -> rist
recipes.description: {{infobox object |name = |image = Image:.png |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |skillreq = Beermaking |objectsreq = Malted Wheat |producedby = Quern }} Wheat grist is ground up malted wheat. It is used to make wort for Weißbier. The quality of the grist is soft-capped by the player's cooking skills. Like other powders, if you drop grist on the ground you will lose it.

recipes.title: Wort
recipes.materials: Water (5 liters), Wheat Grist x5, Hops Cones x2; Bucket (empty)
recipes.how: To make Wort, you need the following things. *Cauldron, with at least 5L of Water in it and a branch worth of fuel so it can be lit. *2x Hops Cones *5x Wheat Grist *An empty Bucket Open and light up your Cauldron, keep the Cauldron's window open, and craft your Wort. This will fill your Bucket with 5L of Wort. This can then be poured into a Wine Barrel, which if left to ferment for one week will turn into Weißbier. Category:Liquids
recipes.description: Wort must be put in a Wine Barrel to ferment into Weißbier.

recipes.title: Candle
recipes.how: The skill Candle Making, Beeswax and Any String are required in order to craft a wax candle. Craft > Candle
recipes.description: A candle can be added to the Miner's Helm or Candelabrum, and a candle is required for the Birthday Cake. Formula for quality appears to be (9*waxQ + stringQ)/10.

recipes.title: Board
recipes.how: Right click on a log while having either a saw or bone saw equipped and the option "Make Boards" appears. A bone saw makes only 4 boards, while a metal saw makes 6. However, it is often easier to get a high quality bonesaw than it is to get an equal quality metal saw, so sometimes a bone saw is still desirable when you want quality boards. Your Carpentry skill acts as a softcap for board quality. The formula for board quality is as follows: Q''n ''= <math>\sqrt{sawql * lumberql}</math> and if (Carpentry > Q''n'') then BoardQuality = Qn if (Carpentry < Q''n'') then BoardQuality = (Q''n'' + Carpentry) / 2
recipes.description: The quality of the boards depends on the quality of the tree, the quality of your saw, and your carpentry skill level.

recipes.title: Bucket
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Bucket
recipes.description: Buckets are used to collect and move up to ten liters each of liquid products. Buckets can store honey, milk, water, wort, grapejuice, vinegar, and wine. It cannot be used to store or carry beer, tea, or rennet. Note that when stored in a bucket, Grape Juice and Wine do not age as they do when contained in a barrel, thus making them the container of choice for long-term wine storage.

recipes.title: Clogs
recipes.how: Craft > Clogs Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor
recipes.description: You can find your first clogs in the newbie chest when creating a new character. Clogs: 20 Hitpoints, Armor class 0/7 (Quality 5)

recipes.title: Shield
recipes.how: Craft > Shield Category:Equipment
recipes.description: Allows the player to use the Shield maneuver. If you use the shield maneuver, you must have a shield equipped, otherwise you will have no defense at all. If you un-equip the shield, your maneuver will automatically change back to Dodge , and you will have to set it back to Shield manually next time you equip a shield. The quality of a shield does not affect anything.

recipes.title: Woodfish
recipes.how: You need to equip a sharp tool to make a woodfish. Craft > Tools > Fishing Gear > Woodfish The quality of the woodfish depends on your carpentry skill.
recipes.description: A woodfish is a fishing lure, which is used together with a fully equipped fishing pole to catch fish.

recipes.title: Ink
recipes.how: In order to craft ink you need an empty Jar, a container filled with at least 1.0 liter of Water and Inkweed in your inventory. Craft > Potions & Herbs > Ink Category:Liquids
recipes.description: Cartography allows your character to draw a local map of the area you are in. You need to have a quill, ink and parchment in your inventory to draw a map.

recipes.title: Cheese Tray
recipes.how: Requires Cheesemaking, uses 1 board to produce one Cheese Tray. Craft > Tools > Cheese Tray
recipes.description: A cheese tray can be filled with 4 curd to produce cheese. The quality of the cheese tray plays a role in the quality of the cheese after it ages. Quality of cheese tray is softcapped by your carpentry skill.

recipes.title: Rennet
recipes.how: Craft > Cooking > Ingredients > Rennet In order to craft rennet you need an empty jar, intestines and at least 0.3 liters of vinegar in your inventory. Furthermore, you need a cauldron to make rennet. Add water and fuel to the cauldron, light it and execute the recipe. Category:Liquids
recipes.description: Rennet is used to make Curd, which is required to produce Cheese.

recipes.title: Cylinder Hat
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Cylinder Hat Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: Cylinder Hat: +1 Industry, +5 Charisma +18 Charisma at Q16

recipes.title: Linen Cloth
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Material > Linen Cloth Approach a loom by left or right clicking it, then begin crafting.
recipes.description: ==How to Acquire== Craft > Cloth Making > Material > Linen Cloth Approach a loom by left or right clicking it, then begin crafting.

recipes.title: Merchant's Robe
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Capes and Cloaks > Merchant's Robe
recipes.description: It doesn't really signify a merchant. The robes moreso signify a person has either great wealth, or a great industry in the silk business. Merchant Robes have a form of "pocket" that increases inventory space by 1 horizontal row: the wearer's inventory will become 5x4, or 5x6 with a backpack.<ref>See the image here for an example. In this case, the person is wearing a backpack, and thus has a 5x6 inventory.</ref> At quality 10, the robe gives +10 Intelligence and + 15 Charisma when worn.

recipes.title: Nettle Pants
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Nettle Pants
recipes.description: Pants made from stinging nettles. {| border="1" ! colspan="4" style="background: #ffdead;" | Nettle Pants at quality 10 |- style="background: beige" ! align="center"| Unarmed Combat Bonus ! align="center"| Survival Bonus ! align="center"| Agility Penalty |- | align="center"| +1 | align="center"| +2 | align="center"| -1 |} {| border="1" ! colspan="4" style="background: #ffdead;" | Nettle Pants at quality 11 |- style="background: beige" ! align="center"| Unarmed Combat Bonus ! align="center"| Survival Bonus ! align="center"| Agility Penalty |- | align="center"| +1 | align="center"| +2 | align="center"| -2 |}

recipes.title: Nettle Shirt
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Nettle Shirt
recipes.description: A shirt made from stinging nettles. {| border="1" ! colspan="4" style="background: #ffdead;" | Nettle Shirt |- style="background: beige" ! align="center"| Quality ! align="center"| Unarmed Combat Bonus ! align="center"| Survival Bonus ! align="center"| Agility Penalty |- ! align="center"| 10 ! align="center"| +1 ! align="center"| +2 ! align="center"| -1 |}

recipes.title: Toga
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Capes and Cloaks > Togaaa Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: This garment is only cosmetic and has no special advantage.

recipes.title: Hirdsman's Cape
recipes.how: You need a loom to make a hirdsman's cape. Craft > Cloth Making > Capes and Cloaks > Hirdsman's Cape Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: While anyone with the proper skills can make it, this item can only be worn by a member of the the hird, a special rank appointed by the chieftain of a village, or the chieftain and the lawspeaker themselves.

recipes.title: Vapntreyiu
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Vapntreyiu
recipes.description: A Vapntreyiu is a unique armor that gives defensive stats, but can be worn underneath defensive armor (like Leather Armor or Bronze Plate). A quality 10 Vapntreyiu has 75Hitpoint, AC 10/10. A quality 30 Vapntreyiu has 225 Hitpoint, AC 17/17. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Hitpoints | style="text-align: center;"|Armor Class |- | style="text-align: center;"|8 | style="text-align: center;"|60 | style="text-align: center;"|8/8 |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"|75 | style="text-align: center;"|10/10 |- | style="text-align: center;"|30 | style="text-align: center

recipes.title: Necromancer's Cloak
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Capes and Cloaks > Necromancer's Cloak Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: To obtain a necromancer's cloak you must either have the skill Murder, or know someone with it. You have to have the skill to craft the cloak but not to wear it. It takes 4 Yarn and 4 Ash to create, and it gives a bonus of +5 INT.

recipes.title: Ranger's Cape
recipes.materials: Linen Cloth x10, Silk Cloth x1
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Capes and Cloaks > Ranger's Cape
recipes.description: A cape that forms part of the Ranger's set of clothing. Increases Stealth and Perception. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 300px;" | style="text-align: center;"|'''Quality''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Stealth''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Perception''' |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 |- | style="text-align: center;"|40 | style="text-align: center;"| +20 | style="text-align: center;"| +20 |- | style="text-align: center;"|90 | style="text-align: center;"| +30 | style="text-align: center;"| +30 |}

recipes.title: Ranger's Pants
recipes.materials: Linen Cloth x10, Silk Cloth x1
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Ranger's Pants
recipes.description: A pair of pants that form part of the Ranger's set of clothing. They increase Stealth and Exploration., {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 300px;" | style="text-align: center;"|'''Quality''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Stealth''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Exploration''' |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +5 | style="text-align: center;"| +5 |- | style="text-align: center;"|40 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 |- | style="text-align: center;"|90 | style="text-align: center;"| +15 | style="text-align: center;"| +15 |}

recipes.title: Ranger's Shirt
recipes.materials: Linen Cloth x10, Silk Cloth x1}}A shirt that forms part of the Ranger's set of clothing. Increases Stealth.
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Ranger's Shirt
recipes.description: A shirt that forms part of the Ranger's set of clothing. Increases Stealth. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|'''Quality''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Stealth''' |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 |- | style="text-align: center;"|40 | style="text-align: center;"| +20 |- | style="text-align: center;"|90 | style="text-align: center;"| +30 |}

recipes.title: Silk Filament
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Material > Unravel Silk Cocoon
recipes.description: Once you have your Cocoons, prepare a Cauldron water (at least 1 litre) and branches, and light it. Activate the Cauldron, and navigate to your Unravel Silk Cocoon command. Note that having max Nature will make this part of the process much more rewarding, as you will receive two filaments instead of one. Also, make sure you have enough inventory space when unraveling, as silk filaments are lost forever if dropped. The quality of the filaments equals the quality of the cocoons regardless of the quality of the cauldron or water used, softcapped by the cube root of (sewing*farming*dexterity).

recipes.title: Silk Thread
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Material > Silk Thread Category:String
recipes.description: Silk thread is a form of string and is created by using silk filaments which come from silkworm cocoons. The cocoons are generated as described on the silkworm page. Silk cloth and several crafted items are created from the thread.

recipes.title: Bandit's Mask
recipes.materials: Linen Cloth x2, Blood Stern x5
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Capes and Cloaks > Bandit's Mask
recipes.description: Bandit's Mask: +3 Stealth

recipes.title: Acorn
recipes.how: Acorns can be gathered from an Oak Tree. Put them in a Treeplanter's Pot with 4 Soil and 1 liter of Water to make a planted oak tree. Place a planted oak tree on an Herbalist Table to begin the sprouting process. If it sprouts, you have 8 hours to plant it. If it dies, you must empty the pot and start over again.

recipes.title: Carrot Seeds
recipes.how: Carrot seeds can be initially obtained from drying a Wild Windsown Weed either on a Herbalist Table for 2.6 hours real time, or on a Drying Frame for 16 hours real time. Carrots grow through four stages: *Stage 1: The carrot field cannot be harvested. *Stage 2: Between 8 and 16 hours after planting it can be harvested for one carrot (Regardless of your nature/industry level). *Stage 3:. Between 16 and 24 hours after planting the carrot plantswill have white spots on them and can be harvested for 1 to 3 carrot seeds (Depending on your nature/industry level). *Stage 4: After 24 hours it can be harvested for 1 to 3 carrots (Depending on your nature/industry level).
recipes.description: Carrot seeds are used to grow carrots.

recipes.title: Flax
recipes.how: When harvested it produces one fibre and flax seeds (up to three seeds) depending on the harvester's Nature/Industry belief slider. Flax has the same resources as hemp, except hemp can be also harvested for their buds (Fresh Hemp Bud) Flax is mostly used to produce Plant Fibres, which is used to make herbalist tables, or for making Linen Cloth, which can be used in the making of Torches, Banners, and other clothing. Flax seeds can be placed into Food Troughs and Chicken Coops It is rumored that hemp grows faster than flax (1 hour faster), making flax obsolete.
recipes.description: ==How to Acquire== When harvested it produces one fibre and flax seeds (up to three seeds) depending on the harvester's Nature/Industry belief slider. Flax has the same resources as hemp, except hemp can be also harvested for their buds (Fresh Hemp Bud) Flax is mostly used to produce Plant Fibres, which is used to make herbalist tables, or for making Linen Cloth, which can be used in the making of Torches, Banners, and other clothing. Flax seeds can be placed into Food Troughs and Chicken Coops It is rumored that hemp grows faster than flax (1 hour faster), making flax obsolete.

recipes.title: Fresh Tea Leaves
recipes.how: Harvesting a tea field gives you 2 tea seeds and up to 2 fresh tea leaves depending on your Nature/Industry belief slider.
recipes.description: |image = Image:.png |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |lpgain = 5 |skillreq = Farming |objectsreq = Tea Field |producedby = Nothing }} In order to make tea you need a harvestable tea field to obtain fresh tea leaves.

recipes.title: Fresh Tobacco Leaf
recipes.how: Harvesting a tobacco crop gives you 1 fresh tobacco leaf and up to 3 Tobacco Seeds depending on your Nature/Industry belief slider.
recipes.description: In order to make Tobacco you need a harvestable tobacco crop to obtain a fresh tobacco leaf, which can then be placed on a Herbalist Table to dry into a Cured Tobacco Leaf.

recipes.title: Giant Pumpkin
recipes.how: You must plant a pumpkin seed, wait for the pumpkin to grow to full size and harvest it (6 growing stages). Pumpkins take 5 days to a week to grow. With the Pumpkin in your inventory you can right click the pumpkin and select "Slice". This choice will yield 8 Pumpkin Seeds and 8 Slices of Pumpkin Flesh (which are edible)
recipes.description: A requires a 4 x 4 empty space in your inventory in order to pick it up. If you slice a pumpkin you get 8 Pumpkin Flesh and 8 Pumpkin Seeds of the same quality of the pumpkin. It is notable that pumpkins are the only crop not affected by the nature/industry belief slider.

recipes.title: Hemp
recipes.description: Hemp is a plant that is harvested for Plant Fibres and Fresh Hemp Buds. Each tile harvest will yield 1-3 seeds depending on your Nature/Industry belief slider and drop a plant fibre on the ground. If hemp is harvested at the second to last stage, recognized by orange blooms, harvesting will yield Fresh Hemp Buds which can be dried on an herbalist table and smoked to gain bonus to FEP when eaten. Harvesting at this stage will not yield hemp seeds or plant fibres.

recipes.title: Hemp Seed
recipes.description: Hemp seeds are produced when you harvest a Hemp Plant in it's third stage. Harvesting a Hemp Plant in it's second stage will yield Fresh Hemp Bud but will resut in the loss of the Hemp Seed.

recipes.title: Hemp Seeds
recipes.description: Hemp seeds are produced when you harvest a Hemp Plant in it's third stage. Harvesting a Hemp Plant in it's second stage will yield Fresh Hemp Bud but will resut in the loss of the Hemp Seed. Category:Seeds

recipes.title: Peppercorn
recipes.how: *The original peppercorn can be obtained by drying Wild Windsown Weed on a Drying Frame or Herbalist Table. *Harvesting a tile of planted peppercorn gives you up to 3 peppercorn depending on your Nature/Industry belief slider.
recipes.description: Peppercorn has no use by itself, and must be cooked into Boiled Pepper Drupes, then dried into Dried Pepper Drupes, then ground into Black Pepper before it can be applied to another food to increase it's FEP gain.

recipes.title: Poppy Flower
recipes.how: Harvesting a poppy field gives you 1 poppy flower and up to 3 poppy seeds depending on your Nature/Industry belief slider.
recipes.description: A red flower grown from Poppy Seeds. It can be used to create Boar Tusk Snuff and a Poppy Wobbler fishing lure.

recipes.title: Poppy Seeds
recipes.how: Harvesting a poppy field gives you 1 poppy flower and up to 3 poppy seeds depending on your Nature/Industry belief slider. Poppy seeds can be obtained from Wild Windsown Weed.
recipes.description: Poppy seeds grow into poppy flowers. They are an ingredient needed for Ring of Brodgar Dough.

recipes.title: Poppy Wobbler
recipes.how: You need one Poppy Flower, together with one Bone and one Leather you can craft it. Craft > Tools > Fishing Gear > Poppy Wobbler
recipes.description: The is a Fishing Lure, which is used together with a fully equipped Fishing Pole to catch Fish.

recipes.title: Pumpkin Hat (Jack-o'-mask)
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Hats > Pumpkin Hat Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: This hat is only cosmetic and has no special advantage. Many bandits and griefers, along with anyone that has access to a pumpkin patch, can wear these.

recipes.title: Pumpkin Seeds
recipes.how: Pumpkin seeds can be initially obtained from Wild Windsown Weed. When Pumpkins are large and round, they are ripe and can be harvested for Pumpkin seeds and Pumpkin Flesh.&nbsp Harvesting a pumpkin gives you a Giant Pumpkin. Slicing this pumpkin gives you 8 Pumpkin Slices and Pumpkin Seeds each.
recipes.description: Pumpkin seeds are used to grow pumpkins.

recipes.title: Tea Seeds
recipes.how: Harvesting a tea field gives you 2 tea seeds and up to 2 fresh tea leaves depending on your Nature/Industry belief slider. Tea seeds can be obtained from Wild Windsown Weed.
recipes.description: In order to make tea you need a harvestable tea field to obtain fresh tea leaves.

recipes.title: Wheat Flour
recipes.how: Once you have the required skill(s) and object(s), right-click on the quern you wish to use, wait until your character bumps up against it, and use the commands below to begin making the flour. Craft [C] > Processing&Materials [k] > Cooking&Ingredients [n] > Flour [F]
recipes.description: Wheat flour, made from wheat seeds, is an ingredient in all baked goods. '''Warning: Dropping flour will cause it to dissapear.'''

recipes.title: Wheat Seeds
recipes.materials: Wheat Crop or Wild Windsown Weed
recipes.how: Wheat seeds can be acquired by harvesting wheat growing on a farm. This typically produces 1 to 3 seeds and will leave a unit of straw on the ground, which can be picked up. Like harvesting any crop, in order to harvest wheat, one must have the farming skill. They can also be acquired from Wild Windsown Weeds by drying them on an Herbalist Table or a Drying Frame.
recipes.description: Wheat seeds can be used on a quern to make wheat flour or to feed chickens on a chicken coop

recipes.title: Gauze
recipes.how: Shear a sheep to get a piece of wool. Take that wool to a loom. Select gauze under the craft menu. Craft > Gauze
recipes.description: There are two ways to heal your hitpoints: #Make a gauze and bandage your head. This will continously heal some of your HHP. When the gauze has stopped healing, you will need to replace it to get full HHP. #Leeches heal up to 1 HHP (the middle number), but do damage to your SHP (the first number). They randomly attach to your equipment slots when walking through swamps.

recipes.title: Bone Hook
recipes.how: A bone hook is made from a bone, easily obtain from animals, such as rabbits or chickens. Craft > Tools > Fishing Gear > Bone Hook The quality of the bone hook depends on your survival skill.
recipes.description: The most accessible fish hook is the bone hook, which is used together with a fully equipped fishing pole to catch fish.

recipes.title: Bream
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish is caught from fishing in deep or shallow water of a lake.
recipes.description: A small pinkish fish. Eating a serving of roasted bream fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 3 intelligence, and 2 charisma in FEPs.

recipes.title: Copperbrush Snapper
recipes.materials: Copper Nugget, Silk Filament, Silver Nugget

recipes.title: Eel
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish is caught from fishing in shallow water of a river using a pinecone plug, Woodfish or Rock Lobster lure and in the deep water of a lake.
recipes.description: A long flat fish, which provides 2 filets. Eating a serving of roasted eel fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 4 agility FEPs.

recipes.title: Feather Fly
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Fishing Gear > Feather Fly The quality of the feather fly depends on your survival skill.
recipes.description: The is a fishing lure, which is used together with a fully equipped fishing pole to catch fish.

recipes.title: Fishing Pole
recipes.materials: Branch x2; Fishing Line; Fish Hook; Fishing Lure or Bait
recipes.how: Create a pole by crafting two branches. raft > ools > ishing Gear > ishing Pole ''Now, you need to add a line, a hook, and some kind of lure or bait before you can use the fishing pole.'' '''Fishing Line''' There are two sources of strings that can be obtained early on. The first is spindly taproot which requires the foraging skill. The other is plant fibres which requires you to find flax or hemp in which the farming skill can be used to obtain more. '''Fish Hook''' The most accessible hook is the bone hook which is made from bones easily obtain from animals, particularly rabbits and chickens. '''Fishing Lure or Bait''' Lures are objects crafted and used the same way as baits are. The difference is that lure is most likely not lost after fishing but has a low success rate. The most accessible lures are Feather Flies (block of wood + chicken feather), Pinecone Plug (cone from fir tree + birch bark), Rock Lobster Lure (stone), and Woodfish (block of wood). Bait can either be leeches obtain by walking through certain swamps or earthworms obtain through digging in the forest (although the stamina it takes to dig might cost more hunger than you would replenish through eating the fish). To equip the fishing pole with fishing line and other necessities, left click on the item to select it and then right click on the top box of the fishing pole.
recipes.description: For more information check the Fishing page. s are tools used to catch fish which is one of the staple food source early on for beginners. Be warned that fish is almost always INT food and if you live on a diet of fish you may increase your INT stat ahead of your other stats making it harder to increase them later.

recipes.title: Metal Hook
recipes.materials: Cast Iron Nugget OR Wrought Iron Nugget OR Bronze Nugget OR Steel Nugget

recipes.title: Perch
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish is caught from fishing in deep water of a lake and a river. In both area, regardless the default kind of fish, only perch is caught when Q61 or more hook is used. (***note by player - I just caught 2 perches with fishing pole q10 hook q10, so I guess this Q61 is an old information****(it's correct. it means that with hook q10 you may catch any fish, but with q over 61 only perch. ) Note: Rock Lobster Lure is good for catching Perch. Lower level perch is caught most reliably from a river using a rock lobster at midday during a small moon. (rocklobster+q5 fishing rod, got perch everytime)
recipes.description: Eating a serving of roasted perch fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 4 intelligence FEP.

recipes.title: Pike
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish is caught from fishing in shallow water and deep water of a lake, and can be found in the deep water of a river. This, however, is extremely rare. (but when you found a good spot it's very simple to have it)
recipes.description: A medium grey fish, which provides 2 filets. Eating a serving of roasted pike fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 2 strength, 2 intelligence, and 1 constitution in FEPs.

recipes.title: Pinecone Plug
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Fishing Gear > Pinecone Plug The quality of the pinecone plug depends on your survival skill.
recipes.description: The is a fishing lure, which is used together with a fully equipped fishing pole to catch fish.

recipes.title: Plaice
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish is most likely caught from fishing in shallow water of a lake during the full moon at night. It has also been found in river depths during a full moon using woodfish bait. Also found in lake deeps, using rock lobster lure.
recipes.description: A small yellowish-grey fish renowned for it's perception gain. Eating a serving of roasted plaice fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 1 intelligence, and 3 perception in FEPs.

recipes.title: Roach
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish is caught from fishing in deep water of a river or lake.
recipes.description: A small light blue fish. Eating a serving of roasted roach fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 2 intelligence, and 2 dexterity in FEPs.

recipes.title: Salmon
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish
recipes.description: A medium light grey fish, which provides 2 filets. Eating a serving of roasted salmon fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 2 intelligence, and 3 charisma in FEPs.

recipes.title: Sturgeon
recipes.how: You must have the fishing skill and a fully equipped fishing pole in order to catch fish. Adventure > Fish is caught from fishing in shallow water and deep water of a river and deep water of a lake.
recipes.description: A large fish with one of the few ways for psyche gain, which provides 3 filets. Eating a serving of roasted sturgeon fills 30 units (3%) of your hunger bar and gives 3 strength, and 1 psyche in FEPs.

recipes.title: Blood Stern
recipes.description: A Blood Stern is a flower growing in the forest, where they can be picked. You need at least 1250 PER*EXP to be able to start spotting them in the world.

recipes.title: Cave Clay
recipes.how: Cave Clay is found in shallow water within Caves and appears as a Forage-able item. How many Cave Clay you receive per spawn is dependent on your Nature(0.5x)/Industry(2x) Slider. I found it with 600 exploration parameter. As of November 8th, 2010 every clay has a specific color when kilned into bricks. When Kilned Cave Clay makes Blue Bricks. .
recipes.description: Cave Clay is an ingredient of Bone Clay, which is used to make Porcelain plates and ceramic meat grinders.

recipes.title: Feldspar
recipes.how: These rocks are found in grasslands, mud flats, and other areas, with a moderate Perception * Exploration requirement. They can be harvested with Foraging: when found, right click them and select "Pick". Like Gray Clay, your ability to harvest Feldspar depends on your Nature/Industry slider, with 100% Nature characters only having a 50% chance to successfully harvest one feldspar rock, while 100% Industry charcters have a 100% chance to gather 2 Feldspar. Caution! The Feldspar can be used as Stones so you can pave Tiles and you can shoot with it. The minimum amount of perception ability (perception*exploration) needed to find feldspar is unknown, though there is speculation. Users have claimed the below; {| border="1" ! colspan="3" style="background: #ffdead;" | Minimum Score Chart |- style="background: beige" ! align="center"| Perception ! align="center"| Exploration ! align="center"| Total Score |- | align="center"|30 | align="center"|36 | align="center"| 1080 |- | align="center"|21 | align="center"|46 | align="center"|966 |- | align="center"| 21 | align="center"|42 | align="center"|882 |- | align="center"|22 | align="center"|40 | align="center"|880 |- | align="center"|26 | align="center"|32 | align="center"|832 |}
recipes.description: Feldspar is an ingredient of Bone Clay, which is used to make porcelain plates and ceramic meat grinders.

recipes.title: Four-Leaf Clover
recipes.how: Four-Leaf Clovers can be found on Grassland, Heath and Moor. Get your (Perception x Exploration) over 144 and you will start seeing some.
recipes.description: left|40px Once found, it can be fed to a Mouflon or Auroch to attain Wool or Milk without having to tame the beast. To use, pick up a clover from your inventory and right-click it onto the animal. To sheer a Mouflon, feed it a clover and then right click on it and select 'sheer'. Each clover gives you 1 wool. You have to equip a sharp tool to be able to sheer (Stone Axe works fine). To milk an Auroch, feed it a clover and then pick up a bucket and right-click in on the Auroch. Each clover gives you 2 litres of milk.

recipes.title: Inkweed
recipes.how: Inkweed can be found on Grassland, Heath and Moor. You need the Foraging skill in order to find inkweed. Your Perception*Exploration needs to be a minimum of 250 to be able to start seeing them (and 1000 to be able to see all of them).
recipes.description: left|50px Inkweed is a herb used to make Ink, which is required for map making.

recipes.title: River Pearl Mussel
recipes.how: They can be found in the shallow water of rivers and lakes. you can "Pick" them up in the same manner you would would a stinging nettle or rustroot. They require a certain amount of perception and exploration to see. Generally, you see the mussels around 800 per*exp(i.e 20*40). The quality of the mussel is directly related to the quality of the fish in that area. frame|River Pearl Mussel Category:Content Category:Objects
recipes.description: Raw mussels can be cooked into Boiled Mussels. While boiling, there is a 1% chance for the mussel to contain a River Pearl. Mussels are often found in clusters but more can be seen based on perception and exploration.

recipes.title: Rustroot
recipes.how: Rustroot is a late game herb, so to find rustroots a player must have a PER*EXP of at least 500 (for example, Perception of 20 and Exploration of 25). The chance to find rustroot increases with higher PER*EXP. At a PER*EXP of at least 2000, all rustroots can be seen.
recipes.description: left|60px Rustroot is used to make Rustroot Extract, which is required for Prospecting.

recipes.title: Stinging Nettle
recipes.description: A Stinging Nettle is a flower growing in the forest, where they can be picked. Nettles can be found when Exploration * Perception is 15 (Contrary to popular belief it is not 30). Nettles can be used as string , with the sole exception of nettles not being usable as a fishing line .

recipes.title: Wild Windsown Weed
recipes.reference: All Terrains, most commonly found on Grassland
recipes.how: In order to see the Wild Windsown Weed you need to have a Perception * Exploration score of at least 8-14 (total of 112). They can be found in most terrains, and are often hard to see because of their size. Have been found with as little as 10 Perception, 7 Exploration.
recipes.description: left|75px The wild crop patches known from the second world in Haven & Hearth have been replaced with Wild Windsown Weeds (WWW's), a new wild plant that you can forage for. This wild plant, when dried, converts into a seed of some kind that you can then plant with the Farming skill. The kind of seed you obtain from Wild Windsown Weeds is random and not dependent on the type of terrain on which they are found. However, if you find several Wild Windsown Weeds in the same "area" they will tend to produce the same type of seed.

recipes.title: Gray Clay
recipes.how: *Locate a Gray Clay in outdoor shallow water *Right click the Gray Clay and select Pick frame|Gray Clay in Shallow Water As of November 8th, 2010 every clay has a specific color when kilned into bricks. When Grey Clay is kilned it makes Black Bricks.
recipes.description: Gray Clay is found by foraging in shallow water, with your Exploration * Perception scores modifying your chance to spot them. They are only found in shallow water along river or lake shores, much like River Pearl Mussels. Additionally, your Nature vs Industry belief slider directly affects your gathering capabilities once you find some Gray Clay. At full nature you will have a 50% chance to get a single Gray Clay, while at full industry, you will find two every time. Note that this means it is possible for those characters at full nature to not receive any at all when attempting to pick up Gray Clay. From anonymous's personal experience, gray clay nodes have been found at 306 per*exp with n

recipes.title: Monocle
recipes.materials: Raw Glass x1, Silver Nugget, Silk Thread
recipes.description: Gives 15 PERception at q10. Quality is based only on the Silk Thread and Raw Glass

recipes.title: Spectacles
recipes.materials: Raw Glass x2, Nugget of Any Common Metal
recipes.description: Decent boost to perception. Gives +5 PER at quality 10. +6 PER on q15. +8 PER on q30 +10 PER on q42 Your Smithing level affects the quality of Spectacles crafted.

recipes.title: Wine Bottle
recipes.how: raft > Ceraics > Wine ottle '''Important: to unlock this recipe you must fill your bucket with a wine.'''
recipes.description: Wine Bottles hold up to 0.6 liters of Vinegar, Wine or Weißbier.

recipes.title: Wicker Breadbasket
recipes.description: Q10 adds 4% Hunger Mod, 1% FEP bonus Category:The Symbel

recipes.title: A mess...
recipes.how: *Right Click a wandering Chick from a Heath, Grassland, or Moor to capture it or obtain from a hatched egg in a Chicken Coop *Right Click the Chick and select Wring Neck from the flower menu
recipes.description: A mess... is the useless guts of a Chick which was captured and had its neck wringed. There is currently no known use for this item. Killing the chick by punching it or hitting it with a weapon produces a Dead Chicken.

recipes.title: Bear Cape
recipes.how: raft > eather Working > Bar Cape
recipes.description: A cape and hood fashioned from the hide of a bear, making the wearer stronger. Increases strength and giving hitpoints. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 400px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Strength | style="text-align: center;"|Hitpoints | style="text-align: center;"|Armor Class |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 | style="text-align: center;"|100 | style="text-align: center;"|0/15 |- | style="text-align: center;"|55 | style="text-align: center;"| +23 | style="text-align: center;"|275 | style="text-align: center;"|0/35 |- | style="text-align: center;"|90 | style="text-align: center;"| +30 |

recipes.title: Boar Tusk
recipes.how: Kill a boar, then right click to collect bones on a boar skeleton after skinning and/or butchering the remains. You will receive one or two tusks along with other bone materials of the same quality. Quality is determined by the formula BoarLevel*4+Survival/2.
recipes.description: The tusk from an aggressive boar. Boar Tusk also counts as bone material for recipes.

recipes.title: Bone Material
recipes.materials: Dead Chicken, Dead Cock, Dead Rabbit, Skeleton
recipes.how: Collect bones from the skeletons left behind by slaughtered corpses. Furthermore, you can kill and butcher a rabbit, hen or cock to get bones. Deer Antlers, Boar tusks and Bear Teeth also count as bones, for purposes other than producing Bone Ash, for Bone Clay. pl:Bone Material
recipes.description: You will need the Hunting skill to get bones, which are required by several things, such as Bone Arrows or Bone Hooks. You don't need to have the hunting skill if you find some animal dead.

recipes.title: Chick
recipes.how: There are two possibilities: *Wild flocks of chicken may randomly spawn on Grassland, Moor, or Heath. Right-clicking a chick will cause your character to chase it. The chick will flee, so running is necessary unless you are standing directly next to it. Once caught, it will appear in your inventory. *Build a Chicken Coop for breeding hens and cocks. A fertile Chicken Egg will hatch into a chick, if brooded by a hen inside a chicken coop.
recipes.description: A chick is a young chicken. They seem to disappear after being placed in your inventory or a container for an extended period of time. Some players have noticed a glitch where if you capture a chick and butcher it, you get A mess, but if you release the chicken and kill it (punching, shooting, etc.) on the ground will be a dead chicken. This could be helpful as to not need to wait for chicks to mature to butcher them.

recipes.title: Chicken
recipes.how: *They are found roaming Grasslands, Moors and Heath biomes. From there they can simply be picked up similar to a Rabbit, however they are very slow, and can be caught at Run speed.
recipes.description: The chicken is a domesticated fowl. In the game, it refers mainly to the female chicken. A male chicken is called a cock.

recipes.title: Cock
recipes.how: There are two possibilities: *Wild flocks of chicken may randomly spawn on Grassland, Moor, or Heath. Right-clicking a cock will cause your character to chase it. The cock will flee, so running is necessary unless you are standing directly next to it. Once caught, it will appear in your inventory. *Build a chicken coop for breeding hens and cocks. When a chick is placed inside a chicken coop with sufficient fodder, it will turn into a hen or cock.
recipes.description: A cock, also called a rooster or chanticleer, is a male chicken.

recipes.title: Dead Chicken
recipes.how: To get a dead chicken all you have to do is right click on a Chicken in your inventory and select "Wring Neck"; or you can simply kill a live chicken, which is running around on Grassland, Moor or Heath.
recipes.description: Right-click and select "Butcher" to convert a dead chicken into Raw Chicken Meat, Bone Material, and three Chicken Feathers.

recipes.title: Dead Cock
recipes.how: To get a dead cock all you have to do is right click the Cock in your inventory and select "Wring Neck".
recipes.description: Right-click and select "Butcher" to convert a dead cock into Raw Chicken Meat, Bone Material, and three Chicken Feathers.

recipes.title: Dead Rabbit
recipes.description: A dead rabbit is what appears when you right-click a live Rabbit in your inventory and select "Wring Neck". It occupies a 1x2 space in your inventory (smaller than the 2x2 of the live rabbit), and when butchered it yields one Rabbit Meat, one Bone Material, and one Fresh Rabbit Fur, all of which start at quality 10. may sometimes yield a raw rabbits foot

recipes.title: Rabbit
recipes.how: Rabbits are found in forests of any kind.Right-click on a rabbit to pick it up from the ground. It is recommended to be next to the rabbit before doing so as right-clicking it will cause it to flee from you at running speed (and has been seen to run through structures). If you have space for a 2x2 item the rabbit will appear in your inventory. If you try to shoot it down successfully, or punch it (one punch with 2 unarmed combat will kill it in a hit), it can give 8 LP depending on your tradition/change slider. It is worth noting that if you mess up catching a rabbit, you can end combat by clicking on the sword and olive branch next to the rabbit portrate.
recipes.description: A live rabbit. Also called "Kroda". You can right-click it and select "Wring Neck" to kill it and receive a Dead Rabbit. The Rabbit also has a rare chance to drop a Lucky Rabits Foot, a curiosity that gives a lot of Learning Points.

recipes.title: Rat
recipes.description: Rats are one of the only creatures, alongside Chickens, Rabbits and Toads, that can be fought without them fighting back. This makes them good in helping novices figure out the Combat System. They do not have any byproducts, cannot be wrung by their neck, and can be picked up by right-clicking them. Rats do not flee in combat. Rats can only be found at night Rats can be made into Rat on a Stick. Rats are often used for Ancestral Worship. Category:Content Category:Creatures Category:Objects

recipes.title: Toad
recipes.description: ''Toads'' are the only creature, aside from Chickens, Rabbits and Rats, that can be fought without them fighting back. This makes them good in helping novices figure out the Combat System. They do not have any byproducts, cannot be wrung by their neck, and can be picked up by right-clicking them. Toads are often used for Ancestral Worship. They usually hop around in the forest and whole swarms in swamps. They stop every 5-7 steps, which makes them easy to catch. They also can be used as a curio for learning points by crafting a Enthroned Toad you need to have a foraging level of 40 or higher to get the resources for one. Killing a toad raises your unhappiness by 7-10%. Category:Content Categ

recipes.title: Waterskin
recipes.description: A Waterskin carries water. made with a dried skin. Craft > Tools > Waterskin

recipes.title: Lawspeaker's Hat
recipes.how: This hat gives an amount of INT and CHA, based on quality. Q18 hat = 9 INT and 4 CHA Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: {{infobox object |name = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |image = Image:.png |caption = |type = Equipment, Inventory |skillreq = Lawspeaking |objectsreq = Linen Cloth x10, Yarn x5 |reqby = Nothing }} You can wear this hat only if you have the lawspeaking skill.

recipes.title: Lawspeaker's Robes
recipes.description: {{infobox object |name = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |image = Image:.png |caption = |type = Equipment, Inventory |skillreq = Lawspeaking |objectsreq = Linen Cloth x15, Yarn x5 |reqby = Nothing }} Wearing this item requires you to be Lawspeaker of a Village.

recipes.title: Boar Tusk Helmet
recipes.how: raft > loth Making > ats > Boar Tsk Helmet Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor
recipes.description: A boar tusk helmet is a basic piece of defensive headgear, made from leather and boar tusks. A battle helmet both used by boar hunters,and a small amount of raiders. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Hitpoints | style="text-align: center;"|Armor Class |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"|120 | style="text-align: center;"|5/30 |- | style="text-align: center;"|19 | style="text-align: center;"|228 | style="text-align: center;"|6/41 |- | style="text-align: center;"|20 | style="text-align: center;"|240 | style="text-align: center;"|7/42 |} Hitpoints = Q*12 Armor Class

recipes.title: Fox Hat
recipes.how: Made with a Dried Fox Hide. :raft > Cloth Making > Hats > Fox Hat
recipes.description: Cosmetic. Resembles the Russian Ushanka or English Bearskin in styling.

recipes.title: Hardened Leather
recipes.how: You need a cauldron or clay cauldron with at least 2.5 litres of hot water to harden leather. *Gather the 5 ingredients into your inventory: 3 Leather and 2 Beeswax *Right click a boiling Cauldron or Clay Cauldron to move into position and ready it for use *Select the recipe: Craft > Leather Working > Hardened Leather *Click the "Craft" button
recipes.description: Hardened Leather is leather treated with beeswax to strengthen it. It requires three pieces of Leather and two units of Beeswax, and may be used to create objects like the Mine Hole, Traveller's Sack or Cutthroat Cuirass.

recipes.title: Hide Strap
recipes.how: raft > eather Working > Hide trap Category:String
recipes.description: Hide straps serve as a generic string, created from any dried Hide. The quality of the hide strap created is equal to the hide used, unaffected by attributes or skill values. Hides give 2 straps per inventory slot (e.g. Rabbit Fur gives 2 straps, Dried Hide gives 8 straps, etc). They are not usable as Fishing Lines.

recipes.title: Leather
recipes.materials: Tree Bark, Water, and one of the following: Dried Bear Hide, Dried Hide, Dried Fox Hide, Rabbit Fur, Dried Sheep Skin
recipes.description: Leather is used to produce a variety of equipment. =How to Acquire= The leather working skill is required in order to make leather. Leather is not difficult to make, but its creation is a time-consuming process. *1. Acquire a raw bear hide, raw hide, raw fox hide, fresh rabbit fur, or raw sheep skin. *2. Dry the hide by placing it in a drying frame. *3. Wait for the hide to finish drying. It takes 8 hours (one full in-game day) to dry most furs, the exception being bear furs, which take 16 hours (two full in-game days). *4. Take the dried hide and place it in a tanning tub. *5. Fill a water container, such as a bucket, using either a well or an open water source. *6. Hold the full container

recipes.title: Leather Armor
recipes.how: Craft > Leather working > Lether Armor
recipes.description: Leather armor is a basic piece of defensive chest armor, made from leather. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Hitpoints | style="text-align: center;"|Armor Class |- | style="text-align: center;"|7 | style="text-align: center;"|105 | style="text-align: center;"|20/20 |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"|150 | style="text-align: center;"|25/25 |- | style="text-align: center;"|30 | style="text-align: center;"|450 | style="text-align: center;"|43/43 |}

recipes.title: Leather Boots
recipes.how: Craft > Leather working > Leather boots
recipes.description: Leather boots are basic defensive footwear, made from leather. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 300px;" | style="text-align: center;"|'''Quality''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Hitpoints''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Armor Class''' |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"|100 | style="text-align: center;"|0/20 |- | style="text-align: center;"|40 | style="text-align: center;"|400 | style="text-align: center;"|0/40 |- | style="text-align: center;"|90 | style="text-align: center;"|900 | style="text-align: center;"|0/60 |}

recipes.title: Leather Cloak
recipes.how: Craft > Leather Working > Leather Cloak Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: This leather cloak is only cosmetic.

recipes.title: Leather Pants
recipes.how: raft > eather Working > Leaher Pants
recipes.description: Leather pants are basic pieces of defensive leg armor, made from leather. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Hitpoints | style="text-align: center;"|Armor Class |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"|150 | style="text-align: center;"|25/25 |- | style="vertical-align: top; text-align: center;"|30 | style="vertical-align: top; text-align: center;"|450 | style="vertical-align: top; text-align: center;"|43/43 |}

recipes.title: Newsboy Cap
recipes.how: raft > eather Working > ewsboy Cap Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: One of the 2 (/3?) hats you can start the game with. It is purely cosmetic, so it does not give any bonuses in any manner.

recipes.title: Parchment
recipes.materials: Dried Bear Hide or Dried Fox Hide or Dried Hide or Dried Sheep Skin or Rabbit Fur
recipes.how: Craft > Parchment Any type of hide works, giving 1 piece of parchment for every space it takes in your inventory: Rabbit Fur gives you 1 parchment, where the Dried Hide gives you 4 parchments. Sheep skin is an exception and gives four pieces of parchment instead of two.
recipes.description: You need a blank parchment to draw a new map. You can also use a blank parchment to write messages, it works the same way a Runestone does.

recipes.title: Seedbag
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Seedbag
recipes.description: One single seedbag holds up to 9 seeds.

recipes.title: Traveller's Sack
recipes.how: *Gather the 13 ingredients into your inventory: 5 Hardened Leather and 8 String *Select the recipe: [C]raft > [L]eather Working > Tra[v]elers Sack *Click the Craft button
recipes.description: Travelers Sack takes the spot of one of your hands and increases a player's inventory space by an amount depending on its quality. Up to two may be equipped at once. Sacks below Q<sub>20</sub> will give the player an additional vertical row, while sacks above Q<sub>20</sub> will give both a vertical and horizontal row. Note: String quality does not contribute to sack quality. Only leather quality is used.

recipes.title: Ranger's Boots
recipes.how: Craft > Leather Working > Ranger's Boots Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor
recipes.description: A pair of boots that form part of the Ranger's set of clothing. They provide the most protection of any footwear and increase Agility. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 400px;" | style="text-align: center;"|'''Quality''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Agility''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Hitpoints''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''Armor Class''' |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +5 | style="text-align: center;"|150 | style="text-align: center;"|15/15 |- | style="text-align: center;"|40 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 | style="text-align: center;"|600 | style="text-align: center;"|30/30 |- | style="text-align: center;"|9

recipes.title: Key
recipes.materials: Nugget of Any Common Metal, Key-Mold
recipes.how: Begin by constructing a wall (brickwall or palisade) cornerpost and then right click on the cornerpost to expand the wall. Two free tiles, paved and flanked by cornerposts on both sides make possible a gate. Once a gate has been built, the character (the character laying the final touch on the gate) will receive a key for it.
recipes.description: Right click on a gate to lock or unlock it, provided you have the key.

recipes.title: Key-Mold
recipes.how: Right-click on a piece of clay with a key to create a key-mold.
recipes.description: Key-molds are used to create copies of keys but can only be used once.

recipes.title: Keyring
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Keyring
recipes.description: Keyrings will hold up to 9 keys. The keys are functional as though they were in your inventory, and can be removed at any time.

recipes.title: Block of Wood
recipes.how: Blocks of wood are produced by chopping either a log or removing a stump with a stone axe. Removing a stump provides 4 blocks and chopping a log provides 12 blocks. Now, you can also right-click blocks and select the "Split" option in order to split the blocks into 5 branches each.
recipes.description: |image = Image:.png |caption = |xsize = 2 |ysize = 1 |lpgain = 10 |skillreq = Lumberjacking |objectsreq = Log or Stump |producedby = Stone Axe }} The quality of the blocks is based on the quality of the tree. The quality of your stone axe has no effect..

recipes.title: Log
recipes.how: Right click on a tree, select the "Chop" option to chop it down. Pine, Oak, and Maple Trees give three logs, Fir , and Elm Trees give two logs, while Apple Trees, and most other fruit trees give only one. Afterwards, a stump will remain which can be removed.
