recipes.description: You can chop a log into 12 blocks with a stone axe, or saw it into 4 boards with a bone saw or 6 with an iron saw. You need carpentry to obtain boards.

recipes.title: Bar of Bronze
recipes.materials: Bar of Copper x2, and Bar of Tin
recipes.how: You need an alloying crucible to make a bar of bronze. It must be fueled with charcoal and lit in order to produce bronze bars. Craft > Metal Working > Bar of Bronze
recipes.description: Two bars of copper and one bar of tin makes three bars of bronze. Bronze is a stronger metal than copper and tin, and can be used in the production of many types of tools. In addition, weapons and armor can be crafted from bronze that, while not always as strong as wrought iron or steel equipment, are generally much cheaper and less time-consuming to make. Bars of Bronze can be used to make repairs to plows made out of cast iron, and vice versa. The main hurdle in acquiring bronze is the need for two types of metal, which cannot be procured from a single mine alone. Thus trade is often required in order to acquire sufficient stocks of both metals that may be used in producing bronze.

recipes.title: Bar of Cast Iron
recipes.how: First, one must acquire iron ore, typically by mining it from a mine hole. Since w4, you can now mine in simple caves. Be sure to build supports so you don't get struck by a cave-in.Next, the iron ore must be loaded into an ore smelter. Four units of charcoal should also be added to the smelter by right clicking on it while holding a unit of coal. Finally, light the smelter. After approximately twenty minutes, the process will be complete. Roughly 10% of the iron ore will become bars of cast iron, with the rest becoming stone. This ratio can be modified by adjusting one's Personal Beliefs. Each point toward industry increases the chance by 2%, while each point toward nature decreases it by 2%. ru:Слиток железа
recipes.description: Cast iron, in bar form, can be used to produce a variety of low-grade metal goods. It can also be smelted into wrought iron.

recipes.title: Bar of Gold
recipes.how: Obtained by combining 10x Gold Nugget using the skill Metal Working.
recipes.description: ==How to Acquire== Obtained by combining 10x Gold Nugget using the skill Metal Working.

recipes.title: Bronze Plate
recipes.how: You need to equip a smithy's hammer and you need an anvil to make a bronze plate. Craft > Metal Working > Armor > Bronze Plate Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor

recipes.description: Category:ContentCategory:ObjectsCategory:EquipmentCategory:Sharp ToolsCategory:Weapons

recipes.how: *Gather the 5 ingredients into your inventory: 5 Steel Nuggets *Select the recipe: Craft > The Symbel > Cutlery > Cake Knife *Click the Craft button Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: A steel knife meant especially for serving cake and decorating a Table. The Cake Knife provides a boost when feasting.

recipes.title: Clothier's Thimble
recipes.how: raft > ewelry > lothier's Thimble
recipes.description: A ring in the form of a thimble that protects the all-important fingers, increasing dexterity and sewing. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 300px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Dexterity | style="text-align: center;"|Sewing |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +1 or +2 | style="text-align: center;"| +5 |} ''Requires confirmation of stats''

recipes.title: Frying Pan
recipes.how: You need to equip a smithy's hammer and you need an anvil to make a frying pan. Make it from one bar of common metal. Craft > Tools > Frying Pan
recipes.description: A frying pan. Needed to fry eggs and make pancakes.

recipes.title: Hirdsman's Helmet
recipes.materials: Bar of Wrought Iron, Block of Wood
recipes.how: You need to equip a smithy's hammer and you need an anvil to make a hirdsman's helmet. Craft > Metal Working > Armor > Hirdsman's Helmet Category:Equipment Category:Clothing Category:Armor
recipes.description: While anyone with the proper skills can make it, this item can only be worn by a member of the hird, a special rank appointed by the chieftain of a village. Hirdsmen can be summoned by the chieftain.

recipes.how: raft > ewelry > ccult Ring
recipes.description: A ring containing the knowledge of the hidden. Increases perception and intelligence. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 300px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Perception | style="text-align: center;"|Intelligence |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +8 | style="text-align: center;"| +8 |} ''Requires confirmation of stats''

recipes.title: Plate Armor

recipes.description: is a piece of high-end chest armor. It deflects four times as much damage as it absorbs, making it more durable than other armors. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 300px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Hitpoints | style="text-align: center;"|Armor Class |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"|5000 | style="text-align: center;"|150/75 |- | style="text-align: center;"|40 | style="text-align: center;"|20000 | style="text-align: center;"|300/150 |- | style="text-align: center;"|90 | style="text-align: center;"|50000 | style="text-align: center;"|450/225 |}

recipes.how: raft > ewelry > ing of Brodgar Category:Equipment
recipes.description: A Ring of Brodgar is a highly prized ring that boosts learning ability. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 250px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Effect |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +15% Learning Ability |}

recipes.title: Scythe
recipes.materials: Bar of Bronze, or Bar of Cast Iron, or Bar of Wrought Iron, or Bar of Steel; AND Block of Wood x3
recipes.how: You need to equip a smithy's hammer and you need an anvil to make a scythe. Craft > Metal Working > Scythe Category:Equipment Category:Sharp Tools
recipes.description: Quoting Jorb, "You harvest faster while having the scythe equipped, obviously."

recipes.title: Shovel
recipes.materials: Bar of Bronze, or Bar of Cast Iron, or Bar of Wrought Iron, or Bar of Steel, Block of Wood x3
recipes.how: Craft > Metal Working > Shoel
recipes.description: A shovel can be used with little stamina drain to rapidly dig for clay/sand/soil. Using a shovel to remove tree stumps halves the time compared to using an axe. Equipping a shovel also greatly reduces stamina drain, (cuts the drain to your stamina by half) when using Adventure > Landscaping > Plow Field for those single spots that are too much of a hassle to grab a plow for.

recipes.title: Silver for the Ferryman
recipes.how: raft > ewelry > ilver for the Ferryman
recipes.description: A ring that prevents the wearer from drowning. Essential for those foolish enough to buy the Swimming skill. One use only.

recipes.title: Silver River Goblet
recipes.materials: Bar of Silver, River Pearl x8
recipes.how: *Gather the 9 ingredients into your inventory: 1 Bar of Silver and 8 River Pearls *Select the recipe: Craft > The Symbel > Cups & Goblets > Silver River Goblet *Click the Craft button Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: A luxurious goblet made from silver and decorated with river pearls. Used to decorate a Table to provide a boost when feasting.

recipes.title: Tankard
recipes.how: You need to equip a smithy's hammer and you need an anvil to make a tankard. Craft > Metal Working > 1 = Tankard
recipes.description: A tankard is used for drinking Weißbier from a Wine Barrel or Wine Bottle. That means you cannot drink Weißbier out of a Glass because that makes sense.

recipes.title: Thane's Ring
recipes.materials: Gold Nugget x2, Silver Nugget x2
recipes.how: raft > ewelry > hane's Ring
recipes.description: A Thane's Ring is a ring for those who would lead in battle. It increases melee combat, strength, agility, and charisma. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 500px;" | style="text-align: center;"|Quality | style="text-align: center;"|Melee Combat | style="text-align: center;"|Strength | style="text-align: center;"|Agility | style="text-align: center;"|Charisma |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 | style="text-align: center;"| +10 | style="text-align: center;"| +5 | style="text-align: center;"| +5 |} ''Requires confirmation of stats''

recipes.title: Tin Fly
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Fishing Gear > Tin Fly
recipes.description: The is a fishing lure, which is used together with a fully equipped fishing pole to catch fish.

recipes.title: Boar Tusk Snuff
recipes.how: You need a Poppy Flower and a Boar Tusk to craft boar tusk snuff. The Militia Training skill is required. Craft > Potions & Herbs > Boar Tusk Snuff
recipes.description: By using boar tusk snuff, your Strength is increased temporarily (15 seconds) by adding the Quality level of the Boar Tusk Snuff I.E. Your Strength = 20 Boar Tusk Snuff Quality = 50 Strength is thus = 70 It is just superstition among players, but rumor has it that snorting Boar Tusk Snuff before embarking on an adventure brings you and your party luck. (and makes you high)

recipes.title: Copper Ore
recipes.how: Copper Ore is obtained by mining in a Copper Mine. To turn it into usable metal, it must be smelted in an Ore Smelter.
recipes.description: Copper Ore is a mined piece of rock and mineral from which a Bar of Copper can be gained.

recipes.title: Iron Ore
recipes.how: Iron Ore is obtained by mining in an Iron Mine. To turn it into usable metal, it must be smelted in an Ore Smelter.
recipes.description: Iron Ore is a mined piece of rock and mineral from which a Bar of Cast Iron can be gained.

recipes.title: Silver Ore
recipes.how: Silver Ore is obtained by Mining in a Silver Mine. To turn it into usable metal, it must be smelted in an Ore Smelter.
recipes.description: Silver Ore is a mined piece of rock and mineral from which a Silver Nugget can be gained.

recipes.title: Tin Ore
recipes.how: Tin Ore is obtained by mining in a Tin Mine. To turn it into usable metal, it must be smelted in an Ore Smelter.
recipes.description: Tin Ore is a mined piece of rock and mineral from which a Bar of Tin can be gained.

recipes.title: Filet of Bream
recipes.description: |caption = |hunger = 30 |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |skillreq = N/A |str = 0 |agi = 0 |int = 0 |con = 0 |per = 0 |cha = 0 |dex = 0 |psy = 0 |hurt = 0 }}

recipes.title: Filet of Brill
recipes.description: |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |skillreq = N/A |type = Inventory |objectsreq = Brill |hunger = 30 |str = 0 |agi = 0 |int = 0 |con = 0 |per = 0 |cha = 0 |dex = 0 |psy = 0 |hurt = 0 }} Quality is directly linked to fish Q Gives 2STR 2AGI

recipes.title: Filet of Pike
recipes.description: |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |skillreq = N/A |type = Inventory |objectsreq = Pike |hunger = 30 |str = 0 |agi = 0 |int = 0 |con = 0 |per = 0 |cha = 0 |dex = 0 |psy = 0 |hurt = 0 }}

recipes.title: Fir Bough
recipes.how: Right click on a sufficiently grown fir tree and select the appropriate option. Each fir tree will provide 5 boughs.
recipes.description: Besides their specific use in crafts, fir boughs can double as Branches in crafting; though they aren't usable as fuel. Fir boughs may be used in any construction or recipe that lists "Any Bough" as an ingredient.

recipes.title: High Hat
recipes.how: This is sometimes found in the newbie chest when creating a new character. There are no known ways by which players can create these. Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: |image = Image:.png |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |skillreq = Not Applicable |objectsreq = Not Applicable |producedby = Not Applicable }} A hat to make your character fashionable (or not).

recipes.title: String
recipes.materials: Hide Strap, Plant Fibres, Silk Thread, Spindly Taproot, Stinging Nettle, or Yarn
recipes.description: String is not an actual object, but refers to Hide Strap, Plant Fibres, Silk Thread, Spindly Taproot, Stinging Nettle, and Yarn. Almost anything that requires string can be made with any combination of these objects. An exception is Fishing Line.

recipes.title: Earthworm
recipes.how: Digging in Forest Terrain has a chance to get an earthworm. Adventure > Dig
recipes.description: Earthworms can be equipped on a Fishing Pole to be used as Bait. Note that bait, unlike lures, must be replaced after each attempt at catching a fish. However, they greatly increase the chance of catching a fish on each try.

recipes.title: Tea
recipes.materials: Green Tea Leaves x2, or Black Tea Leaves x2; AND Tea Pot; Mug
recipes.description: Tea fills up about 45% stamina per full mug. Drinking tea does not decrease hunger like drinking water does. Tea also grants you "The Warmth of Tea" Buff which gradually increase your LP learning rate as the duration pass to an additional base 20%. The quality of tea affects the rate at which the learning rate increases each interval; this in turn increases the highest point of learning rate you can obtain when the buff is as its peak. There is no difference between green tea and black tea.

recipes.title: Acre Clay
recipes.reference: By hand digging on a mudflat
recipes.how: To obtain Acre Clay you have to dig in a mudflat that's close to a quality clay node. As of November 8th, 2010 every clay has a specific color when kilned into bricks. When Acre Clay is Kilned it makes Red Bricks.
recipes.description: Acre Clay is one of the variations of Clay. Any of these variations work as the regular clay did. This is one of the two types of clay that can be made into bricks.

recipes.title: Ball Clay
recipes.how: To obtain Ball Clay you have to dig in shallow water that's close to a quality clay node.
recipes.description: Ball Clay is one of the variations of Clay. Any of these variations work as the regular clay did, and all of these variations can be made into bricks. When Ball Clay is burnt in the kiln it makes Yellow Bricks.

recipes.title: Earthenware Platter
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: raft > The ymbel > lates > Earthenware Platter Mold the clay into an unburnt earthenware platter and place into a kiln with at least 6 branches worth of fuel. It takes approximately 20 minutes to fire the plate. Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: s are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you and how many FEPs food provides. Q26 gives 13,3% Hunger Mod, 1% FEP Mod

recipes.title: Jar
recipes.how: To make a jar, you need the pottery skill and 3 blocks of clay. Craft > Ceramics > ar *Use the craft menu to mold the clay, and then fire the unburnt jar in a kiln with 4 branches loaded. *In about 20 minutes you will get an empty jar.
recipes.description: Jars can hold 1.0 L of Rennet, Ink or Rustroot Extract.

recipes.title: Pipe
recipes.how: To make a pipe, you need the pottery skill and 2 blocks of clay. You also need to have discovered tobacco. Use the craft menu to mold the clay, and then fire the unburnt clay pipe in a kiln. You will need a minimum of 4 branches as fuel. To gain a pipe recipe, you need to discover "harvesting" of tobacco/hemp and picking it up once leaves/buds are dried from herbalist table. Although cured hemp is used with the pipe. You can only gain skill from removing Tobacco from the Herbalist table. Craft > Ceramics > Ppe
recipes.description: Pipes can be equipped in the alternate head slot. They can be filled with Cured Hemp Buds or Tobacco and lit, causing the character's pipe to emit smoke for 5 minutes, as well as turning them into a small light source. *Smoking Cured Hemp Buds produces the "In The Fog" buff. The longer the character smokes, the more potent the buff is (whether quality affects duration or effect is unknown). "In The Fog" increases FEP gain from foods. *Smoking Tobacco reduces Travel Weariness and gives the "Smoke & Fire" buff that temporarily increases Psyche.

recipes.title: Porcelain Plate
recipes.how: raft > The ymbel > lates > Porcelain Plate Then burn the unburnt porcelain plate in a Kiln. Cooking time in the kiln is roughly 35-40 minutes, i.e. 7-8 Branches worth of fuel. Category:Content Category:Objects Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: A can be placed on a Table, and affects the hunger modifier and FEP bonus when feasting. At q15 it gives 30% food modifier and 3 FEP bonus

recipes.title: Raw Glass
recipes.how: Dig on beach tiles to get sand. Burn sand in a Kiln to get raw glass. At least four branches required. Category:Content Category:Objects
recipes.description: You need raw glass for several glass products.

recipes.title: Sand
recipes.how: Adventure > Dig Sand can be acquired by digging at the beach. Beaches are found around some lakes. The character will continue extracting sand until either the inventory is full or the player instructs the character to do otherwise.
recipes.description: Sand is used to create Raw Glass. Sand, like many powders, disappears when dropped on the ground.

recipes.title: Soil
recipes.how: Adventure > Dig Soil is dug from any forest tiles or grassland. It takes approximately 3.5 seconds to dig up one unit of soil. Note that if you find a source of high quality soil, the quality will decrease the more you dig there. However, it will restore itself to its original value over time.
recipes.description: Soil is a resource that is used in creating tree saplings to plant for trees. It is also one of the primary factor if the quality of trees planted. Because trees are used to create many different structures and furniture, the quality of trees are very important thus high quality soil is valued and may be traded to people that lacks access to them. As of now, there is no known way in increasing the quality of soil other than finding it. However, an easy way to find quality soil is by foraging, as quality of foraged plant is directly relational to the soil it is on. For more information on finding high quality soil, go to this link. '''Update''' As of the the creation of World 4, soil nodes ha

recipes.title: Stoneware Vase
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: raft > The ymbel > ecorative Items > Stoneware Vase Place the Unburnt Stoneware Vase into the Kiln and fuel with at least 7 branches worth of fuel. Firing takes approximately 35 minutes. Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: Stoneware Vases are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you and how many FEPs food provides.

recipes.title: Bone Saw
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Bone Saw Category:Equipment
recipes.description: A bone saw is required to make boards from a log. You get 4 boards from each log instead of the 6 boards you get with a saw. The quality of a bone saw is affected by survival and capped by bone quality. The formula for bone saw quality is Q1 = ((5 * boneQuality) + branchQuality)/6 if (Survival > Q1) then BoneSawQuality = Q1 if (Survival < Q1) then BoneSawQuality = (Q1 + Survival)/2 Note: To craft a bone saw, one must have discovered bones (and branches)

recipes.title: Stone Axe
recipes.how: Craft > Tools > Stone Axe Category:Equipment Category:Sharp Tools Category:Weapons Category:Mining Tools
recipes.description: |image = Image:.png |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |lpgain = 7 |skillreq = Primitive Tools |objectsreq = Branch, Stone |producedby = Nothing }} A stone axe is made from 1 stone and 1 branch. It can be used to chop down a tree, shear a sheep, for combat or mine in a cave. It increases the effectiveness of chipping stone and destroying objects Damage: 100 base Quality of axe is = (BranchQ+StoneQ)/2 and softcapped by Survival skill.

recipes.title: Torch
recipes.how: Craft [C] > Torch [r].
recipes.description: {{infobox object |name = |image = Image:.png |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 1 |skillreq = Primitive Tools (?) |objectsreq = Branch, Linen Cloth }}

recipes.title: Vile Vapor of Vandalism
recipes.how: Right-click on the smoking footprint (specifically, the footprint itself) and select track to collect a limited number of scents. Category:Content Category:Scents Category:Objects
recipes.description: This is a foul scent left behind whenever someone moves onto a personal claim or village claim which they are not authorized to be on and destroys objects such as cupboards or structures such as trees. It can be used to try to find the perpetrator and the perpetrator's hearth fire.

recipes.title: Milestone
recipes.materials: - Initial Milestone:, Stone x150, Rope x4,, A Beautiful Dream! x5
recipes.how: uild > onstruction > Miestone Once you have an initial milestone constructed, you can build mini milestones by right clicking the initial milestone. Continue right clicking the new milestones to create more. Link them, and you have a path-findable road. If you use a sign to connect the last stone to itself, it will make a character following the trail turn around at the end and start following the trail in the opposite direction instead of stopping at the end of the trail. You can no longer extend this trail. If you want to extend it, you'll have to destroy the last milestone. Then the new last milestone will have gone back to default last milestone behaviour.
recipes.description: Road Building allows you to construct milestone objects that you or another Hearthling can then follow.

recipes.title: Silk Cloth
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Material > Silk Cloth
recipes.description: The final step in the silk process. It's also the hardest. You need a total of 10 Silk Threads, which takes 100 Silk Filaments. The Silk Cloth may be used for the Cylinder Hat, Merchant's Robe, Ranger's Cape, Ranger's Shirt, or Ranger's Pants, as well as to make a Silk Napkin or Silk Tablecloth (Symbel items)

recipes.title: Bone Glue
recipes.how: requires 10 pieces of bone material, and is made using a cauldron. There needs to be at least 5.0 liter of hot water in the cauldron. Right click the boiling cauldron, then: Craft > Potions & Herbs > Bone Glue
recipes.description: You need bone glue to build and later to repair a battering ram, which cannot be used until the glue has dried.

recipes.title: Rock Lobster Lure
recipes.how: The rock lobster lure is made with one stone. Craft > Tools > Fishing Gear > Rock Lobster Lure The quality of the rock lobster lure depends on your survival skill.
recipes.description: The rock lobster lure is a fishing lure, which is used together with a fully equipped fishing pole to catch fish.

recipes.title: Party Hat
recipes.materials: Parchment x2, Ink (0.5 L), Poppy Flower x4, String x2
recipes.how: Craft > Cloth Making > Hats > Party Hat Category:Equipment Category:Clothing
recipes.description: Party Hat: +10 Charisma at Quality 10.

recipes.title: Antler Steak Cutlery
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: raft > The ymbel > utlery > Antler Steak Cutlery Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: Antler Steak Cutlery are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you and how many FEPs food provides.

recipes.title: Black Pepper
recipes.materials: Dried Pepper Drupes x5 (Quern) or Dried Pepper Drupes x10 (Peppermill)
recipes.how: You need a Quern to make a black pepper. raft > Cooing > Blck Pepper
recipes.description: is a food ingredient that increases the FEP gain of foods that it is sprinkled over. Apply to food by picking up and right clicking on target food. {| border="1" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" style="width: 200px;" | style="text-align: center;"|'''Quality''' | style="text-align: center;"|'''FEP increase''' |- | style="text-align: center;"|10 | style="text-align: center;"|50% |}

recipes.title: Boiled Pepper Drupes
recipes.how: You need a cauldron with at least 0.5 litres of hot water to make a boiled pepper drupes. raft > Cooing > Igredients > Boiled epper Drupes Category:Objects
recipes.description: Boiled Pepper Drupes are a food ingredient created by boiling Peppercorn. The boiled drupes must be then dried into Dried Pepper Drupes on an Herbalist Table and either ground into Black Pepper or put in a Peppermill before they can be used. The quality of the boiled pepper drupes is softcapped by the sqrt(Farming skill * perception). The quality of the cauldron and water used also determines the final outcome.

recipes.title: Charcoal
recipes.how: Charcoal is produced by burning blocks of wood and boards in a kiln. Both provide 2 pieces of charcoal. You need to burn 2 branches or 1 board to make charcoal from boards or 4 branches to make it from blocks of wood. About 17 minutes to make charcoal from blocks. Boards, blocks of wood and <u>charcoal</u> can also be used as fuel.
recipes.description: Charcoal is used as fuel for the alloying crucible, ore smelter and finery forge, and is an ingredient in the steelmaking process. Coal acts as two branches worth of fuel when using kilns, ovens, smelters, crucibles, cauldrons, and any other wood-fueled objects.

recipes.title: Coiled Rope Hotpad
recipes.how: Gather the 3 ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: Craft > The Symbel > Coasters, Trivets & Hotpads > Coiled Rope Hotpad Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: A hotpad used for decorating a Table to provide a small FEP boost when sitting and eating at it. Q26 gives 3% of hunger modifer and 0% of FEP bonus.

recipes.title: Dried Pepper Drupes
recipes.how: Place the boiled drupes on the herbalist table and wait roughly two real-life days.
recipes.description: A food ingredient required to make Black Pepper. Created by drying Boiled Pepper Drupes on an Herbalist Table.

recipes.title: Drinking Horn
recipes.materials: Boar Tusk, Nugget of Any Common Metal x2
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: Craft > The Symbel > Cups & Goblets > Drinking Horn Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: Drinking Horns are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you and how many FEPs food provides.

recipes.title: Druid's Cloak
recipes.description: |image = image:Druidscloak.png |caption = |xsize = 1 |ysize = 2 |lpgain = Unknown |skillreq = Unknown |objectsreq = Unknown |producedby = Developers }}

recipes.title: Fresh Rabbit Fur
recipes.how: Butcher a Rabbit Corpse. Category:Hides
recipes.description: You can dry fresh rabbit fur on a drying frame to get a rabbit fur.

recipes.title: Hard Metal Cutlery
recipes.materials: Cast Iron Nugget x4, or Bronze Nugget x4, or Steel Nugget x4
recipes.description: Q28 gives 4.8% Hunger Mod, 1% FEP Mod Q40 gives 13.5% Hunger Mod, 2% FEP Mod Category:The Symbel

recipes.title: Intestines
recipes.how: Intestines are left behind after a player butchers certain animals. Animals that leave intestines after being butchered are: cow/auroch (8); sheep/mouflon (4); fox (2); boar (4); deer (6); bear(8). The quality of intestine obtain is based on the level of the animal as well as the type of animal as well. From lowest to highest are Auroch/Mouflon, Fox, Boar, Deer, Bear.
recipes.description: Intestines are used with the Sausage Making skill to produce many goods. You may also need the Basic Mechanics skill so you can make the Meat Grinder. They are also used in the cheese making process, as a component of rennet. You can now also use intestines as fishing bait.

recipes.title: Linen Napkin
recipes.description: s are used for decorating a Table to provide a small FEP boost when sitting and eating at it. A quality 10 or below reduces Hunger by 0.8% and gives a 0% FEP bonus. A quality 65 napkin reduces Hunger by 6.3% and gives a 2% FEP bonus. Category:The Symbel

recipes.title: Linen Tablecloth
recipes.description: s are used for decorating a Table to provide a small FEP boost when sitting and eating at it. Q65 gives 6.7% Hunger Mod, 0% FEP mod Q51 gives 4.2% Hunger Mod, 0% FEP mod Category:The Symbel

recipes.title: Metal Mug
recipes.materials: Nugget of Any Common Metal x5
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: Craft > The Symbel > Cups & Goblets > Metal Mug Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: Metal Mugs are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you.

recipes.title: Metal Plate
recipes.materials: Nugget of Any Common Metal x8
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: Craft > The Symbel > Plates > Metal Plate Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: Metal Plates are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you and how many FEPs food provides. Q10 gives 7% Hunger Mod, 1% FEP Mod Q40 gives 10.1% Hunger Mod, 3% FEP Mod

recipes.title: Metal Saucière
recipes.description: Q66 gives 31.6% Hunger mod, 5% FEP Mod Q33 gives 14.4% Hunger Mod, 3% FEP Mod Q9 gives 14.7% Hunger Mod, 1% FEP Mod Q2 gives 7.1% Hunger Mod, 0% FEP Mod The effect it has on the Hunger Mod is not as strong when you have other objects on the table, so a 66ql Metal Sauciere on a table with 100% Hunger bonus will reduce it by 31.6% but on a table with 59% Hunger bonus will reduce it by 14.5%. (Tested results) ''Know any more qualitites? Edit them in!'' Category:The Symbel

recipes.title: Peppermill
recipes.materials: Block of Wood, Nugget of Bronze or Nugget of Iron or Nugget of Steel
recipes.how: raft > The ymbel > ecorative Items Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: A is used to create Black Pepper. A quality 47 gives 16.7% base hunger modifier and 2% base food event bonus when placed on a Table.

recipes.title: Poppy Garland
recipes.materials: Straw x8, Poppy Flower x6, Blood Stern x4
recipes.description: Poppy Garlands are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you and how many FEPs food provides.

recipes.title: Raw Bear Hide
recipes.how: Raw Bear Hides are obtained by killing a bear and skinning it while holding a sharp object such as a stone axe.But be patient the hide is drying for 16 hours. When dried on a drying frame and tanned in a tanning tub, bear hide provides the largest quality leather of all the animals. Alternatively it can be used to make a Bear Cape or Chieftain's Hat. Obtaining a bear hide can be dangerous as bears are the most dangerous animals of the game and will attack unprovoked. Category:Hides
recipes.description: The quality of a is affected by survival and the sharp tool quality (ie.: stone axe).

recipes.title: Raw Meat
recipes.how: can be acquired from animal corpses such as Chickens, Rabbits, or Boars. Equip any Sharp Tool, then right click on the corpse and select Butcher from the action list. '''Eating raw meat (chicken atleast) takes HHP away! '''<u>'''Eat too much and you die!'''</u>
recipes.description: can be roasted over a Fire, or made into Sausages.

recipes.title: Raw Sheep Skin
recipes.how: Equip any sharp tool, then right click on the corpse and select "Skin" from the action list. Category:Hides
recipes.description: The quality of a is affected by survival and the sharp tool quality (ie.: stone axe). Put this onto a Drying frame to create a Dried Sheep Skin.

recipes.title: Soft Metal Cutlery
recipes.materials: Nugget of Any Common Metal x4
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: raft > The ymbel > utlery > Soft Metal Cutlery Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: Soft Metal Cutlery are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you. Q10 gives 3.6% Hunger Mod, 0% FEP Mod Q19 gives 6.5% Hunger Mod, 0% FEP Mod Q41 gives 9.5% Hunger Mod, 0% FEP Mod

recipes.title: Stitched Leather Coaster
recipes.description: * Q12 gives 0.8% Hunger Mod and 0% FEP Bonus * Q1024 gives 150% Hunger Mod, 70% FEP bonus quality formula: (q leather+q string)/2 + perception lvl Category:The Symbel

recipes.title: Tobacco
recipes.how: You need a Cured Tobacco Leaf and a Quern to make tobacco. Quality is softcapped by sqrt(perception * farming). Craft > Potions & Herbs > Tobacco
recipes.description: Smoking tobacco reduces Travel Weariness, gives the "Smoke & Fire" buff that temporarily increases Psyche, and creates a small light source around your player.

recipes.title: Water
recipes.materials: Any of the following: Kuksa, Waterskin, Waterflask, Bucket, Wine Barrel
recipes.how: Water can be acquired from a Well or from any water tile. Category:Liquids
recipes.description: Water is a resource that requires a container that can hold liquid and is used for many forms of crafting. Most water is of quality 10 but occasionally there will be areas that will have higher quality. There is no other way of having high quality waters other than discovering these areas. Because if this, high quality water is fairly valued and may be traded as commodity if the other party does not have access or has not discovered high quality water. Water is one factor in obtaining high quality trees. For more information on finding high quality water, see Finding high quality water, clay, and soil. Water can also be used to regain stamina much like Tea, except it will drain hunger at the

recipes.title: Wood Plate
recipes.how: Gather the ingredients into your inventory and execute the recipe: Craft > The Symbel > Plates > Wood Plate Category:The Symbel
recipes.description: Wooden Plates are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you.

recipes.title: Wooden Cup
recipes.description: Wooden Cup are placed on Tables for decorative purposes as well to reduce how full food makes you.

recipes.title: Grape Juice
recipes.how: Put Grapes into a Winepress use it. This will put grape juice in the press (shown as a bar); two full pressings will fill the press with juice. With an empty bucket selected, right-click the press to fill the bucket with juice.
recipes.description: |image = Image:.png |caption = |xsize = 2 |ysize = 2(Bucket) |skillreq = Winemaking |objectsreq = Grapes |producedby = Wine Press }}

recipes.title: Grape Seeds
recipes.how: Eat raw grapes, or press them in a winepress to get grape seeds.
recipes.description: You must have winemaking to plant grape seeds.

recipes.title: Vinegar
recipes.how: To produce vinegar, you need a winepress and a lot of grapes (50 grapes for 10l). Put the grape juice into a wine barrel and wait for one ingame week till the juice turns into wine. Wait one week again and you have vinegar. Vinegar can be stored in Wine Bottles. Category:Content Category:Objects Category:Liquids
recipes.description: You need vinegar to make rennet.
